title: tanacetum aureum var. heimerlii
intro: Tanacetum aureum var. heimerlii, commonly known as Golden Tansy, is a perennial plant species in the daisy family, native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and fern-like foliage. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to repel insects and relieve digestive issues. The health benefits of Golden Tansy include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, making it effective in treating conditions such as fever, colds, and menstrual cramps. Golden Tansy can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal medicine to support the immune system and promote relaxation.
